hypothesis: A mixture-IR decoding model augmented with a learned non-linear interaction module that accounts for pairwise inter-molecular spectral interference will outperform the current linear-additive baseline in identifying and predicting the correct molecules in multi-component mixtures (≥3 components) with strong intercomponent interactions—especially under imbalanced compositions—across both simulated and ATR-IR experimental data, as evidenced by statistically significant gains in Top‑1/Top‑10 accuracy and Tanimoto similarity.
